doc_title: Scalable Pre-training of Large Autoregressive Image Models

items.fact: The paper cites [13, 64, 75] for the revolution of large language models.
items.evidence_span: with the recent revolution of large language models (LLMs) [13, 64, 75].

items.fact: The paper cites [79] for the Transformer architecture.
items.evidence_span: highlighting the potential synergy between the autoregressive pre-training and this architecture.

items.fact: The paper cites [18, 76] for prior work in autoregressive representation learning for images.
items.evidence_span: Similar approaches have been investigated in audio [57] and images [18, 76].

items.fact: The paper cites [29] for the success of Vision Transformers (ViT).
items.evidence_span: The Transformer architecture has also been successfully used in other domains, in particular, computer vision with the success of the Vision Transformers (ViT) [29].

items.fact: The paper cites [43, 75] for recent advances in LLM pre-training.
items.evidence_span: and recent advances in LLM pre-training [43, 75].

items.fact: The paper cites [66] for the prefix attention mechanism.
items.evidence_span: First, instead of restricting the self-attention to be fully causal as is typically the case for LLMs, we adopt a prefix attention, as in T5 [66].

items.fact: The paper cites [19] for the token-level prediction head.
items.evidence_span: Second, we use a heavily parameterized token-level prediction head, inspired by the heads used in contrastive learning [19].

items.fact: The paper cites [24, 45, 74] for stability-inducing techniques.
items.evidence_span: Overall, the training of AIM is similar to the training of recent LLMs and does not rely on any stability-inducing techniques [24,45,74] that supervised [24,74] or selfsupervised [5,58] methods need.

items.fact: The paper cites [29] for the linear embedding method.
items.evidence_span: Input images are split into non-overlapping patches and embedded linearly following Dosovitskiy et al. [29].

items.fact: The paper cites [41] for the normalized pixel-level regression loss.
items.evidence_span: By default, we adopt a normalized pixel-level regression loss similar to He et al. [41].

items.fact: The paper cites [32] for the DFN dataset.
items.evidence_span: We pre-train our models on the DFN dataset introduced by Fang et al. [32].

items.fact: The paper cites [33] for the DataComp 12.8B dataset.
items.evidence_span: This dataset is composed of a larger collection of 12.8B image-text pairs [33] filtered from Common Crawl.

items.fact: The paper cites [28] for the Vision Transformer architecture.
items.evidence_span: As the backbone, we adopt the Vision Transformer architecture (ViT) [28].

items.fact: The paper cites [64, 75] for the common practice in language modeling.
items.evidence_span: we follow the common practice in language modeling and we prioritize expanding width rather than depth $[64,75]$.

items.fact: The paper cites [65] for the PrefixLM formulation.
items.evidence_span: referred to as the prefix, as a context for predicting the remaining patches following the PrefixLM formulation of Raffel et al. [65].

items.fact: The paper cites [79] for sinusoidal positional embeddings.
items.evidence_span: We add sinusoidal positional embeddings [79] to the input patches before the transformer and before the MLP head.

items.fact: The paper cites [50] for the attention pooling operation.
items.evidence_span: we find that our approach, along with other generative approaches like MAE, benefit more from an attention pooling operation [50] placed before the linear classifier.

items.fact: The paper cites [1] for the attention pooling operation.
items.evidence_span: Other works [1, 74, 84] have adopted this attention pooling to improve performance with minimal increase in parameters or FLOPs.

items.fact: The paper cites [74] for the attention pooling operation.
items.evidence_span: Other works [1, 74, 84] have adopted this attention pooling to improve performance with minimal increase in parameters or FLOPs.

items.fact: The paper cites [84] for the attention pooling operation.
items.evidence_span: Other works [1, 74, 84] have adopted this attention pooling to improve performance with minimal increase in parameters or FLOPs.
